- Day 2: All-staff ceremony (60m) + breakouts (30m) for team-level action mapping.
- Day 3: Publish OKRs, exec notes, and PM action items to Confluence + JIRA tickets created.

Draft company-level OKRs (starting point; each objective needs 1–2 owners and 3–4 measurable KRs)
Objective A — Make inference predictable and cheaper for production customers
- KR1: Reduce median hosted API token cost by 18% quarter-over-quarter (measurement: $/1k tokens). (owner: Ravi P.)
- KR2: Achieve 99.95% availability on Redwood Dedicated for >95% of customer traffic slots. (owner: SRE lead)
- KR3: Deliver 2 model quantization profiles in Optimize recommendations with validated regressions <2% on our eval suite. (owner: Applied ML)

Objective B — Improve developer onboarding and time-to-first-success
- KR1: Cut median onboarding time to first successful call from sign-up to working example from 72h -> 24h. (owner: DevX)
- KR2: Publish 6 new quickstart templates (including private-deploy) and measure adoption rate >= 30% within 45 days. (owner: Developer Experience)
- KR3: Lower developer-reported friction score in NPS mini-survey from 3.6 -> 4.2. (owner: Product)

Objective C — Enterprise readiness and compliance acceleration
- KR1: Complete SOC2 gap list with remediation owners and close 80% of items. (owner: Security & Compliance)
- KR2: Ship private-deploy turnkey installer v0.2 and validate in 2 customer pilots. (owner: Private Deployments)
- KR3: Publish formal SLA matrix with latency targets and pricing for Dedicated. (owner: Product & Finance)

Notes on metrics: baseline numbers are TBD — engineering leads/sdrs to add current values when they fill the one-pager. If baseline missing, tag as BLOCKER in #clarity-week.

All-Staff run-of-show (60 min) — condensed choreography
- 0:00–0:05 Welcome + context (CEO) — 3 lines: why this quarter matters, 1 quick win callout, the expectation of decisions.
- 0:05–0:15 Executive highlights (x3 leaders, 3 min each) — focus: risks, where we need cross-team help, and a single ask.
- 0:15–0:30 Interactive prioritization poll (Menti/Slido) — company-level tradeoffs: latency vs cost vs reliability. Quick breakout into 6 rooms.
- 0:30–0:45 Breakout reports (30s per room) + one-sentence commitments.
- 0:45–0:55 Key operational decisions (Ops/Infra) — what we are committing to buy/stop/hire.
- 0:55–1:00 Close + next steps (VP Ops) — where to find the published OKRs, action owners, and timeline.

Prep checklist (must be actionable and small)
- Owners: fill OKR one-pager by Day -1. Template in linked_artifacts.
- SRE/Infra: provide rough capacity delta for Dedicated requests by Week 1 Day 4.
- Finance: provide commit-level cost delta for proposed Dedicated capacity by Week 2 Day 2.
- DevX: ensure quickstart templates listed and a link is ready in the all-staff pre-read.
- Comms: prepare 1-page pre-read and a short internal blog post draft.

Decisions that need to happen in sprint (explicit)
- Approve two Dedicated capacity tiers for pilot customers (Finance + Infra).
- Greenlight private-deploy installer pilot for 2 named accounts (Sales + Legal sign-off).
- Prioritize infra hiring for SRE vs. Applied ML infra contractor (headcount team decision).

Risk register (top 6)
- Risk: Missing baselines -> measurement noise. Mitigation: owners required to fill baselines or escalate; otherwise tag KR as 'qualitative'.
- Risk: Leadership bandwidth — execs double-booked. Mitigation: 2 rehearsals, asynchronous sign-offs allowed for small wording changes.
- Risk: Too many OKRs (scope creep). Mitigation: cap to 6 company KRs; every extra KR needs 2-exec sign-off.
- Risk: Resource commits slip. Mitigation: Finance to post cost delta and an approval deadline (Week 2 Day 2).
- Risk: All-staff interactivity fails (poll tool outage). Mitigation: backup Google Forms + manual tally.
- Risk: Customer escalations during sprint consuming capacity. Mitigation: designate incident hot-seat rotations (SRE) and freeze non-critical ship decisions for 48 hrs.

Facilitator notes / scripts (rough)
- Opening: prompt the CEO to tell a 60-second customer story that connects to the first objective (cost predictability). Real story wins.
- Poll phrasing: "If you could spend one resource on Q2 to deliver the most impact for customers, would you pick: a) Latency, b) Cost, c) Reliability, d) Developer UX".
- Breakout prompt: "Name one thing your team will stop doing this quarter to free up capacity for the chosen KR". Capture in a shared doc.

Post-sprint handoff (72 hours after all-staff)
- Publish: final OKRs, executive one-pagers, and the recording.
- JIRA: PMs create ticket per KR with acceptance criteria and a 45-day short milestone. Tag: OKR-Q2-2025.
- Follow-up: PM-run retro after 21 days to check signals and drift.

Open questions / TODOs (to resolve in pre-sprint)
- Who is the final approver for infra headcount — CFO or VP Eng? (TODO: Confirm by Day -2)
- Confirm guest list for the All-Staff HQ location (hybrid logistics).
- Baseline numbers: infra and finance to fill by Day -1 or mark as qualitative.

Quick exec update talking points (for 10-min slot)
- 1: Progress snapshot: what we shipped last quarter that materially changed unit economics.
- 2: What we will commit to this quarter and why it's safe to do so (risk + mitigation).
- 3: One thing we need everyone to stop doing that frees capacity.

Appendix: templates and links (placeholders)
- OKR one-pager template: see linked_artifacts.
- All-staff pre-read checklist: 1 page (Comms to paste into Confluence).
- Recording + deck template: GitHub / decks repo link.

Rough owners for next steps
- Sprint lead & facilitator: Ava Chen (company-ops)
- OKR consolidation: Ravi Patel (Product)
- All-staff logistics & comms: Maya Gonzalez (People & Comms)
- Technical runbooks & infra capacity: Liam O'Rourke (Infra)

title: Decision Accelerator: 48hr Runbook (v0.1)
preview:
Purpose:
Quick reference runbook for rapid company prioritization during a condensed exec + all-hands window. This doc is intentionally terse — a working checklist and decision ledger to be used over a 48-hour cycle where leadership needs to converge on OKRs, finalize exec notes, and align the all-hands slate.

Read this first (TL;DR):
- Day 0: leadership sync to surface top 6 candidate priorities and one critical constraint per candidate.
- Day 1 morning: narrow to top 3 priorities + agree measurable signals and owners.
- Day 1 afternoon: draft exec update and all-hands framing; public comms owner assigned.
- Day 2: finalize OKR wording, commit resource shifts, publish internal one-pager, rehearse all-hands short calls.

Scope and constraints:
- Timeboxed: entire flow must complete within 48 hours unless escalation flagged.
- Resource moves limited to shifting existing headcount/effort; no new hires in this window.
- Any cross-org dependency must have a named escalation owner and fallback plan.

Initial candidate priority slate (raw inputs from teams):
1) Latency SLO stabilization across hosted API (owner: Priya Singh, eng-serving-runtime) — constraint: infra capacity for canary.
2) Dedicated capacity onboarding automation (owner: Jonah Kim, product-dedicated) — constraint: docs + billing infra.
3) Private deployment compliance baseline (owner: Elena Voss, security-and-compliance) — constraint: legal signoffs.
4) Optimize pilot for customer X (owner: Samir Rao, solutions-eng) — constraint: budget window Q2.
5) SDK multi-region release (owner: Ava Chen, developer-experience) — constraint: test infra.
6) Cost telemetry rollup (owner: FinanceBI) — constraint: instrumentation completeness.

Decision criteria (ranked):
- Business impact (customer ARR impact, renewals, sales enablement) — high weight
- Risk reduction (security/compliance/regulatory) — high weight
- Cost/latency ROI (unit economics improvement within quarter) — medium weight
- Implementation velocity (can ship meaningful progress in 6 weeks) — medium weight
- Cross-org complexity (number of teams needing changes) — negative weight

48hr cadence (detailed):
- Day 0 (kickoff, 0-4 hours):
  - 30m leadership intake (CEO, CTO, Head of Product, Head of Ops, Head of Sales). Bring 1 slide per candidate, explicit constraint.
  - 60m scoring + forced-rank exercise (use spreadsheet linked).
  - Output: top 3 prioritized with provisional owners and signals.
- Day 0 (4-24 hours):
  - Working groups formed for each top-3 priority (<=3 owners each).
  - Draft measurable signals and a single OKR-style line for each priority (Owner drafts).
  - Communications lead drafts exec update outline and all-hands 12-minute narrative.
- Day 1 (24-36 hours):
  - Exec rehearsal (30m): align talking points, fallback messages for Q&A.
  - Finalize OKR wording and minimal roll-forward plan (3 milestones, one-week check-ins).
- Day 2 (36-48 hours):
  - Publish internal one-pager to company (Confluence + Slack).
  - All-hands slide deck final check and presenter assignments.
  - Post-mortem slot scheduled for +1 week to capture improvements.

Communications checklist (internal + external):
- Internal: Confluence one-pager (owner: Communications) — must include explicit rationale, metrics, and who is impacted.
- Slack: #company-announcements + targeted team pings for affected groups.
- External (only if customer-facing change): coordinate with Sales/CS for co-communication.

Template: OKR candidate -> final wording checklist
- Title (one line):
- \"Why now\" (2 sentences max):
- Impact metrics (1-3 metrics and target delta):
- Owner(s) and backup:
- Milestones (3 checkpoints):
- Risk & fallback (1 line):

Draft OKRs (rough prose, to be tightened):
OKR A: Reduce 95th-percentile token latency for hosted-api by 20% for sequences >1024 tokens by end of Q2; Owner: Priya Singh; signals: p95 latency pipeline, error rate on long sequences.
OKR B: Automate 80% of Dedicated onboarding steps (from sign-up to reserved pool allocation) to reduce average onboarding time from 10 days to 48 hours; Owner: Jonah Kim.
OKR C: Complete SOC-aligned baseline for Private deployments for mid-market customers; Owner: Elena Voss.

All-hands framing (12 minutes):
- Opening (2m): CEO quick context.
- What we prioritized and why (4m): 3 bullets with impact stakes and how we will measure progress.
- What it means for teams (4m): concrete changes, who to talk to, immediate asks.
- Close & Q&A (2m): pointer to follow-up doc and how to raise concerns.

Escalation ladder (if vote fails or cross-org blocking):
- Step 1: Named owner escalates to Head of Ops (Maya Patel).
- Step 2: Ops to pull in CTO or Head of Product for tie-break.
- Step 3: CEO decision if >48 hours of unresolved critical blocker.

Risk register (quick hits):
- Risk: canary capacity shortage -> Mitigation: fallback to reduced throughput variant + customer SLA notice.
- Risk: legal delay on Private baseline -> Mitigation: postpone customer targeting window; ship monitoring-first baseline.
- Risk: SDK multi-region bugs -> Mitigation: staggered rollout with feature flags.

Open questions (to resolve in first 8 hours):
- Which sales-identified customers must be excluded from any canary disruption? (Action: Samir to list top 5)
- Are there any active enterprise contract clauses that prevent rapid config changes? (Action: Elena to check legal)
- Budget headroom to shift a contractor from Optimize to Dedicated work? (Action: FinanceBI)

Immediate next actions (ownership and short notes):
- Priya: prepare p95 baseline numbers + short risk doc for canary (due: +12h).
- Jonah: draft automation milestone list + required infra tickets (due: +12h).
- Elena: confirm legal gating items + propose minimal compliance baseline (due: +18h).
- Communications (Diego): initial exec update draft + all-hands slide skeleton (due: +18h).
- Ops (Maya): schedule all-hands rehearsal and lock presenter list (due: +24h).

Post-runbook playbacks and artifacts to publish after 48h:
- Confluence one-pager (finalized)
- OKR entries pushed to planning board (owner: Product Ops)
- Decision ledger export (sheet) with timestamps and vote records
- Post-mortem notes and improvements backlog

Notes to reviewers (internal tone):
- This is a working template: keep bullets tight, flag anything that increases cross-org friction beyond 3 teams.
- When drafting exec text, prefer clarity over optimism; be explicit about tradeoffs.
- If you add a new candidate priority after Day 0, document why it was missed initially and who requested it.

Appendix: quick links and helpers
- Prioritization scoring sheet: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1abc-example
- OKR template page: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/PROD/okrs-template
- Decision ledger template (copy): https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/ledger-copy

Version history:
- v0.1 (this file): initial 48hr runbook sketch — Maya Patel (2025-02-18)

Unresolved (notes to self):
- Consider a lightweight voting mechanism (Slack emoji + tally) for quick tie-breaks; need rules to avoid bias.
- Confirm who owns external comms for customer-impacting changes (Sales lead + Solutions eng).

End of doc.

title: Backstage All-Hands: Roleplay Cues, Incident Playbook & Micro-OKR Sync
preview:
Purpose:
This is the backstage runbook for the next company All-Hands (Townline). It merges three things into a single working doc: (1) the rehearsal script and speaker cues for the executive slate, (2) an incident playbook specific to interruptions during the live meeting, and (3) a compact micro-OKR alignment checklist for post-all-hands follow-up. Primary aim is to reduce last-minute ambiguity and ensure all contingencies are owned.

Audience:
- Execs and presenters (stage-facing)
- MC / emcee and producer (AV & comms)
- Company-ops & comms leads responsible for post-handoff actions
- SRE on-call and infra contacts for live demos

Context and constraints:
- Format: 60 minutes total (45 min presentation + 15 min live Q&A).
- Hybrid event: in-office studio + live stream to global offices; pre-recorded fallback available for demos.
- Primary focus: company priorities for the quarter (micro-OKRs), progress highlights, and two short product demos.
- Acceptable risk posture: tolerate minor delays (<= 3 min), avoid demo-only stalls longer than 5 min—switch to recorded clip.

Run of show (compact):
- 0:00 – 0:03 — Emcee welcome, housekeeping, SLOs for meeting (timeboxes, how to ask questions, recording notice)
- 0:03 – 0:10 — CEO 5-7m opening + 2m “Call to focus” (top 3 company priorities)
- 0:10 – 0:22 — Product direction + 1 demo (live) — product lead presents (10m)
- 0:22 – 0:30 — Engineering highlights: latency & cost wins (8m)
- 0:30 – 0:36 — Customer stories / CS spotlight (6m)
- 0:36 – 0:44 — Ops & reliability: new Dedicated commitments and SLA changes (8m)
- 0:44 – 0:52 — Research & Optimize brief (8m)
- 0:52 – 1:00 — Live Q&A moderated by emcee (8m) + closing

Pre-show checklist (producer):
- Confirmation 48h prior: all speakers have slide decks + 2 rehearsed talking points each.
- A/V test 30m prior: video, mic, captioning, room lighting.
- Slack channel #allhands-live created and monitored by comms.
- Recorded demo clips uploaded and verified (3 quality checks) and reachable at the linked_artifacts URLs.

Speaker cues (brief and actionable):
- CEO (opening): "Lead with one customer story, then pivot to the top three company priorities — why they matter this quarter." Keep to 5–7 minutes. If time slips, cut the final anecdote.
- Product lead (demo 1): "Start with the one-sentence value prop, 60s live flow, then the 90s critical measurement (latency/cost). If demo fails, stop and cue recorded clip C1 immediately."
- Engineering presenter (latency/cost): "Show the before/after chart (p95 latency, token cost). Keep technical detail to 2 slides. If the live metrics dashboard times out, share static screenshot S1 and call out planned follow-ups."

Roleplay snippets for rehearsal (use during the 3x run-throughs):
- Emcee: "We have 10 minutes until stage — sound check ready? Producer: confirm. SRE: can you hold for a potential demo rollback?"
- Presenter: "If I lose audio mid-demo, do not attempt to keep talking — pause and hand to emcee to transition to recorded."

Incident playbook (interactive, stage-specific):
- Failure class A — presenter device disconnect / remote feed lost:
  - Immediate action: Emcee pauses, announces a 60s break while producer confirms fallback.
  - Escalation: Producer pings infra lead (SRE on-call) + comms.
  - Recovery options (choose one): (a) swap to recorded demo C1; (b) switch to next speaker; (c) quick Q&A served by emcee.
  - Messaging line: "We're switching to a short pre-recorded clip while we resolve an AV hiccup — thanks for bearing with us."

- Failure class B — live metrics dashboard times out or shows incorrect data:
  - Immediate action: Engineering presenter displays static screenshot S1 and summarizes the expected state.
  - Post-meeting action: Create a postmortem ticket (INFRA-1429) and assign to infra lead.

- Failure class C — live-stream outage (global viewers can't see the feed):
  - Immediate action: Confirm whether office audience still running. If so, continue and note to comms for follow-up. If not, pause and switch to audio-only broadcast.
  - Escalation: Communications lead drafts a short post to #allhands-live and external comms channel.

Ownership matrix during incident:
- Producer: executes fallback clip, coordinates with AV vendor, updates emcee.
- Emcee: owns live messaging tone and timing.
- SRE on-call: triage feed or infra issues; if unrelated to meeting infrastructure, declare and hand to engineering on-call.
- Comms: prepares post-event message and internal notes summarizing what viewers missed.

Micro-OKR sync (post-all-hands cadence):
- Within 24h: company-ops publishes the top 3 micro-OKRs called out in the meeting to the company Confluence page (link in linked_artifacts).
- Within 72h: each executive owner creates a 2-line plan for the quarter (owner, key metric, one immediate deliverable) and links a ticket (JIRA key).
- Within 2 weeks: product & eng create combined rollout schedule for the two showcased demos (dates, owners, rollback plan).

Follow-up template (to be sent in the all-hands Slack thread):
- TL;DR of top 3 priorities, links to slides and recorded demos, list of owners & micro-OKR bullets, where to comment or push follow-ups (Confluence / Jira).

Risk matrix (brief):
- Demo failure: Likelihood medium; impact high. Mitigation: pre-record + rehearsals + producer ownership.
- Cross-team misalignment on micro-OKR definitions: Likelihood medium; impact medium. Mitigation: 72h exec 2-line plan + follow-up alignment meeting.
- Live-stream outage: Likelihood low; impact high. Mitigation: audio fallback + comms playbook.

Decision log (live during rehearsal):
- D1: If demo fails within first 30s: always swap to recorded clip. (Owner: Producer)
- D2: If cumulative run-of-show slips by >5 minutes: cut internal segment (Research brief) and move to async deep-dive. (Owner: CEO)

Post-handoff checklist (company-ops):
- Publish meeting minutes and time-stamped video within 24h.
- Open micro-OKR tracking board and tag exec owners.
- Create follow-up tickets for any infra incidents with links to the meeting timecode.

Appendix: rehearsal schedule (recommended)
- T-3 days: dry run with all speakers, 60m. Record run so presenters can self-review.
- T-1 day: tech rehearsal (A/V only), 30m. Check captions and stream ingest endpoints.
- T-2 hours: final check with emcee, producer, and AV.

Appendix: canned messaging snippets for comms
- If we used a recorded clip: "We switched to a pre-recorded demo to keep the meeting on schedule. The team will publish a follow-up note with details and an annotated video clip for those who want the full live context."
- If stream outage: "Our primary stream encountered an intermittent outage — we will post a recording and a recap within 24 hours. Thanks for your patience."

Open questions to resolve this week (owner in parentheses):
- Do we lock the agenda at T-48h or allow one additional slide from each exec? (Ava)
- Which demo specifically has the highest risk and therefore must be pre-recorded as a primary replacement (not fallback)? (Product lead + SRE)
- Confirm captions vendor SLA and whether we need a backup (Comms)

Notes / freeform (for iteration):
- Consider a 2-minute pre-recorded "state of the world" montage to reduce dependence on a single live opening.
- If we plan to highlight Dedicated capacity pricing changes, prepare a one-slide FAQ to drop into the Slack thread to reduce immediate support churn.

Tracking links + immediate actions (today):
- COMMS-241: create #allhands-live channel and pin the follow-up template. (Owner: Priya)
- INFRA-1429: pre-create postmortem ticket for potential demo infra failures. (Owner: Ethan)
- GH PR 58: ensure recorded demo asset is merged into the internal-demos repo. (Owner: Marta)

This is a living document — please make edits inline, add additional contingencies you expect, and flag any gaps in the ownership matrix. The goal is a single source of truth so rehearsals can be minimal and the live meeting resilient.

- 0:30 – 0:36 — Customer stories / CS spotlight (6m)
- 0:36 – 0:44 — Ops & reliability: new Dedicated commitments and SLA changes (8m)
- 0:44 – 0:52 — Research & Optimize brief (8m)
- 0:52 – 1:00 — Live Q&A moderated by emcee (8m) + closing

Pre-show checklist (producer):
- Confirmation 48h prior: all speakers have slide decks + 2 rehearsed talking points each.
- A/V test 30m prior: video, mic, captioning, room lighting.
- Slack channel #allhands-live created and monitored by comms.
- Recorded demo clips uploaded and verified (3 quality checks) and reachable at the linked_artifacts URLs.

Speaker cues (brief and actionable):
- CEO (opening): "Lead with one customer story, then pivot to the top three company priorities — why they matter this quarter." Keep to 5–7 minutes. If time slips, cut the final anecdote.
- Product lead (demo 1): "Start with the one-sentence value prop, 60s live flow, then the 90s critical measurement (latency/cost). If demo fails, stop and cue recorded clip C1 immediately."
- Engineering presenter (latency/cost): "Show the before/after chart (p95 latency, token cost). Keep technical detail to 2 slides. If the live metrics dashboard times out, share static screenshot S1 and call out planned follow-ups."

Roleplay snippets for rehearsal (use during the 3x run-throughs):
- Emcee: "We have 10 minutes until stage — sound check ready? Producer: confirm. SRE: can you hold for a potential demo rollback?"
- Presenter: "If I lose audio mid-demo, do not attempt to keep talking — pause and hand to emcee to transition to recorded."

Incident playbook (interactive, stage-specific):
- Failure class A — presenter device disconnect / remote feed lost:
  - Immediate action: Emcee pauses, announces a 60s break while producer confirms fallback.
  - Escalation: Producer pings infra lead (SRE on-call) + comms.
  - Recovery options (choose one): (a) swap to recorded demo C1; (b) switch to next speaker; (c) quick Q&A served by emcee.
  - Messaging line: "We're switching to a short pre-recorded clip while we resolve an AV hiccup — thanks for bearing with us."

- Failure class B — live metrics dashboard times out or shows incorrect data:
  - Immediate action: Engineering presenter displays static screenshot S1 and summarizes the expected state.
  - Post-meeting action: Create a postmortem ticket (INFRA-1429) and assign to infra lead.

- Failure class C — live-stream outage (global viewers can't see the feed):
  - Immediate action: Confirm whether office audience still running. If so, continue and note to comms for follow-up. If not, pause and switch to audio-only broadcast.
  - Escalation: Communications lead drafts a short post to #allhands-live and external comms channel.

Ownership matrix during incident:
- Producer: executes fallback clip, coordinates with AV vendor, updates emcee.
- Emcee: owns live messaging tone and timing.
- SRE on-call: triage feed or infra issues; if unrelated to meeting infrastructure, declare and hand to engineering on-call.
- Comms: prepares post-event message and internal notes summarizing what viewers missed.

Micro-OKR sync (post-all-hands cadence):
- Within 24h: company-ops publishes the top 3 micro-OKRs called out in the meeting to the company Confluence page (link in linked_artifacts).
- Within 72h: each executive owner creates a 2-line plan for the quarter (owner, key metric, one immediate deliverable) and links a ticket (JIRA key).
- Within 2 weeks: product & eng create combined rollout schedule for the two showcased demos (dates, owners, rollback plan).

Follow-up template (to be sent in the all-hands Slack thread):
- TL;DR of top 3 priorities, links to slides and recorded demos, list of owners & micro-OKR bullets, where to comment or push follow-ups (Confluence / Jira).

Risk matrix (brief):
- Demo failure: Likelihood medium; impact high. Mitigation: pre-record + rehearsals + producer ownership.
- Cross-team misalignment on micro-OKR definitions: Likelihood medium; impact medium. Mitigation: 72h exec 2-line plan + follow-up alignment meeting.
- Live-stream outage: Likelihood low; impact high. Mitigation: audio fallback + comms playbook.

Decision log (live during rehearsal):
- D1: If demo fails within first 30s: always swap to recorded clip. (Owner: Producer)
- D2: If cumulative run-of-show slips by >5 minutes: cut internal segment (Research brief) and move to async deep-dive. (Owner: CEO)

Post-handoff checklist (company-ops):
- Publish meeting minutes and time-stamped video within 24h.
- Open micro-OKR tracking board and tag exec owners.
- Create follow-up tickets for any infra incidents with links to the meeting timecode.

Appendix: rehearsal schedule (recommended)
- T-3 days: dry run with all speakers, 60m. Record run so presenters can self-review.
- T-1 day: tech rehearsal (A/V only), 30m. Check captions and stream ingest endpoints.
- T-2 hours: final check with emcee, producer, and AV.

Appendix: canned messaging snippets for comms
- If we used a recorded clip: "We switched to a pre-recorded demo to keep the meeting on schedule. The team will publish a follow-up note with details and an annotated video clip for those who want the full live context."
- If stream outage: "Our primary stream encountered an intermittent outage — we will post a recording and a recap within 24 hours. Thanks for your patience."

Open questions to resolve this week (owner in parentheses):
- Do we lock the agenda at T-48h or allow one additional slide from each exec? (Ava)
- Which demo specifically has the highest risk and therefore must be pre-recorded as a primary replacement (not fallback)? (Product lead + SRE)
- Confirm captions vendor SLA and whether we need a backup (Comms)

Notes / freeform (for iteration):
- Consider a 2-minute pre-recorded "state of the world" montage to reduce dependence on a single live opening.
- If we plan to highlight Dedicated capacity pricing changes, prepare a one-slide FAQ to drop into the Slack thread to reduce immediate support churn.

Tracking links + immediate actions (today):
- COMMS-241: create #allhands-live channel and pin the follow-up template. (Owner: Priya)
- INFRA-1429: pre-create postmortem ticket for potential demo infra failures. (Owner: Ethan)
- GH PR 58: ensure recorded demo asset is merged into the internal-demos repo. (Owner: Marta)

This is a living document — please make edits inline, add additional contingencies you expect, and flag any gaps in the ownership matrix. The goal is a single source of truth so rehearsals can be minimal and the live meeting resilient.

title: 50-day Huddle Cadence & All-Hands Prep Manifest
preview:
Purpose
This doc collects the operational plan and simple playbook for the next 50 days leading into the Q2 all-hands and exec update. It is intentionally lightweight: a cadence, owners, key messages to test, and a short OKR draft that we can iterate in weekly huddles.

Audience
- Execs (read-ahead)
- People leads + ICs running demos
- Comms & GTM for alignment

Summary timeline (50-day window)
- Day 0 (2025-02-10): Draft circulated (this doc)
- Week 1: Leadership read-ahead + feedback (2025-02-11 to 2025-02-17)
- Weeks 2-4: Team huddles (weekly), consolidate one-pagers, capture 3 demos
- Week 5: Exec dry run + comms review
- Day 50 (2025-03-31): All-hands + published exec update

Cadence (practical)
- Weekly 45-minute cross-functional huddle (Mon 09:30 PT) — agenda: progress vs action log, hot blockers, demo signups, message test results.
- Bi-weekly OKR review with exec sponsors (30m)
- Two micro-sessions (30m) for demo rehearsals and QA the week before runway

Who owns what (quick map)
- Program lead: Ava Chen — orchestration, meeting notes, deck collate
- OKR shepherd: Diego Morales — collect metric inputs, draft language
- Comms lead: Priya Kapoor — announcement cadence, townline script
- Demo coordinator: Jasmine Park — slot booking, runbook for presenters
- SRE liaison: Liam O'Neill — readiness checklist (prod demos), incident fallback plan

Draft OKRs (starter language — to be iterated in huddles)
- Objective A: Reduce inference tail-latency variability for hosted API customers
  - KR1: Decrease 95th percentile token-latency by 20% (baseline = internal perf week 2025-01)
  - KR2: Ship two routing fallback policies and enable for top-5 customers by EoQ
  - KR3: Document and roll out KV-cache configuration templates for three canonical workloads

- Objective B: Lower per-token unit cost for common PLG workloads
  - KR1: Evaluate and pilot 2 quantization profiles on staging with perf/regression guardrails
  - KR2: Deploy automatic batch-sizing suggestion in Optimize for top-tier projects
  - KR3: Achieve 8% cost reduction on synthetic workload used by growth team

- Objective C: Improve product reliability & visibility for Dedicated customers
  - KR1: Publish SLA playbook and run a canary with one pilot customer
  - KR2: Add token-level cost traces to Console for dedicated routes
  - KR3: Reduce mean time to recovery (MTTR) for infra incidents by 30% in pilot teams

Draft exec update bullets (one-liners for slides)
- Production: 95p latency down 10% in beta, routing fallback shipped as beta policy
- Cost: pilot quantization reduced synthetic cost by ~6% (early)
- Adoption: 3 Dedicated pilots onboarded, one private deploy contract in negotiation
- Risks: KB cache regressions on long-context streams; mitigation: automated eval + rollback plan

All-hands planning (asks + slots)
- 25-minute leadership narrative (CEO/CTO) — theme: "Serving as the product" (why inference ops matter)
- 3x 8-minute demo slots (serving runtime, Console new traces, Optimize tooling)
- Q&A: crowdsource top-10 questions from team form, moderated by People Ops
- Breakout rooms: product roadmap micro-sessions (followups, 20m)

Prep checklist (immediate next steps)
- [ ] Diego: consolidate metric sources by 2025-02-14 (Jira PLAT-4821)
- [ ] Priya: draft announcement + townline script v0.1 by 2025-02-18
- [ ] Jasmine: confirm demo slots and required infra by 2025-02-21
- [ ] Liam: produce runbook + rollback plan for each demo by 2025-02-24
- [ ] All: add 1 slide each team for the leadership deck by 2025-03-07

Open questions / risks (for weekly huddle)
- Demo stability: which demos absolutely require private-deploy staging vs hosted staging?
- Customer sensitivity: what product info can we share publicly during all-hands about pilots?
- Data: are we confident in metric baselines for latency/cost? (Diego to validate)

Notes to self (informal)
- Keep slides short, favor 1 metric per slide. Execs like crisp signals + change requests.
- Consider adding micro-postmortem slot in huddle if a demo fails — reduces stress and stigma.

Reference links (quick)
- Draft OKR doc: confluence.redwood.company/okrs/q2-2025-draft
- All-hands logistics sheet: drive/sheets/ah-logistics-2025
- Runbook template: github.com/redwood-inference/runbooks/template

title: Nonlinear Onboarding Micro-Pathways — 21-Day Checklist and Hiring Signal Matrix
preview:
Purpose:
This doc provides a compact, nonlinear 21-day onboarding checklist intended for fast-moving, cross-functional hires (engineering, product, and solutions). The goal is to create multiple small, interchangeable ‘micro-pathways’ that let managers and buddies pick the right sequence of activities for each role while preserving consistent hiring signals for early performance assessment.

Scope and audience:
- People Ops: runbooks and templates for onboarding coordinators.
- Hiring managers and buddies: practical day-by-day checklist and communication snippets.
- Recruiting: signal matrix to translate interview feedback into in-role success hypotheses.

How to use this doc:
- Choose a micro-pathway (technical, product, or customer-facing) that best matches the hire.
- Follow the minimum viable checklist (required items) and optionally add stretch items.
- Use the hiring signal matrix at the end to map interview scores to in-role learning goals and 30/60/90 success criteria.

Micro-pathway structure (why nonlinear):
- Not every hire benefits from the same schedule order. Sequencing by cognitive load improves learning: start with low-friction wins (read, observe), move to paired work, then to independent ownership.
- Each week contains 3 required outcomes and 2 optional stretch tasks. Managers can swap tasks between weeks but must keep required outcomes.

21-Day Checklist (required tasks emphasized):
Week 1 (days 1–7): Orientation & low-friction wins
- Day 0 (pre-start): IT access, calendar invites for core recurring meetings, intro email to team (template below).
- Day 1: Orientation (HR overview, benefits), team intro + one 30m buddy shadow session, confirm laptop and dev environment checklist. REQUIRED: access to internal docs and repo read permissions.
- Day 2–3: Docs deep-dive (product overview, architecture map), watch two recorded playbook walkthroughs, pair with buddy on a tiny read-only ticket. REQUIRED: complete security & compliance quick quiz.
- Day 4–5: Phone/home environment checks (VPN, keys), set up 1:1 cadence with manager, complete a 60–90m exploratory pairing session with a peer. OPTIONAL: intro presentation (5–10m) on past work to the pod.
Week 2 (days 8–14): Paired work and small ownership
- Day 8: Shadow calls (if customer-facing), or watch two feature demos. REQUIRED: first paired PR: triage + comment + small patch (non-critical).
- Day 9–11: Rotate through two code reviews or product spec reviews; owner writes 1-page runbook for the area they touched. REQUIRED: submit first PR with tests or submit a product discovery hypothesis doc.
- Day 12–14: Take ownership of a small backlog item end-to-end (deploy gated to staging). OPTIONAL: propose a micro-improvement (perf, docs, tests) and schedule a 15m demo with the buddy.
Week 3 (days 15–21): Independent delivery & measurement
- Day 15: Handoff complete; own the piece in staging and run post-deploy checklist. REQUIRED: deliver a short postmortem-style note on what went well and blockers encountered.
- Day 16–18: Iterate on feedback from week 2 paired work; add improvements, update runbook, and show a demo. REQUIRED: define 30/60/90 goals with manager documented in a shared template.
- Day 19–21: Launch low-risk change to production (if applicable) or lead a customer-facing call; schedule a 21-day retrospective with manager + buddy + 1 peer. OPTIONAL: volunteer to run onboarding for the next hire in this area.

Role-specific micro-pathway examples (templates):
- Technical IC: emphasize dev environment, small PRs, code review rotation, reading model-serving docs, unit test ownership.
- Product PM: emphasis on product teardown, metrics digest, writing a discovery hypothesis, running a small stakeholder alignment session.
- Solutions Engineer: emphasis on customer shadowing, sandbox repro, playbook updates, deployment runbook ownership.

Hiring Signal Matrix (how to translate interviews -> first-21 day goals):
- Signal categories: Technical craft, Communication & stakeholder empathy, Bias for action, Ownership mindset, Learning velocity.
- Scoring guide (interview average): 1–2 = risk (heavy ramp + mentoring), 3 = match (normal), 4–5 = high-confidence (accelerated goals).
Mapping to 21-day expectations:
- Technical craft low (avg ≤2): focus days 1–14 on pairing and shadowing; manager must own a daily 15m checkpoint. Goal: single small PR with tests by day 14.
- Learning velocity high (avg ≥4): compress paired work and add a stretch project for independent delivery before day 21.
- Ownership mindset low: require the hire to write runbooks and handoff notes early and pair with SRE or support for incident triage practice.

Interview Rubric (quick bank suitable for on-site or remote loops):
- Technical probe (engineer): code reading exercise (15m), then debugging follow-up (20m); score on correctness, debugging approach, test mind.
- System design (senior): 30m whiteboard; evaluate trade-off framing, capacity planning thought, SLO awareness.
- Behavioral (all roles): STAR probes around an ambiguous project; score on clarity, stakeholder mapping, impact measurement.
- Role-fit (product/solutions): past deliverable teardown + hypothesis challenge; score on product sense and data use.
Scoring keys: 1 = insufficient, 2 = borderline, 3 = competent, 4 = strong, 5 = exceptional. Add notes field for concrete examples.

Mini-assignment ideas (short, ungraded, can be completed in 4–8 hours):
- Engineering: fix a low-risk bug and add a unit test; include a 1-paragraph explanation of the approach.
- Product: write a 1-page experiment hypothesis with success metrics.
- Solutions: reproduce a customer issue in the sandbox and outline a runbook.
These assignments should be structured so they do not leak proprietary data and can be completed asynchronously.

30/60/90 Signals (manager checklist to track):
- Week 1 signal: Has the hire completed required accesses and a first paired PR/runbook? (Y/N)
- Week 2 signal: Is the hire able to complete independent small tasks with limited review and update docs? (Y/N)
- Week 3 signal: Does the hire present a short demo and propose two measurable improvements? (Y/N)
Use these as inputs to the formal 30/60/90 check-in doc that goes into the HR file.

Communication templates (short snippets):
- Intro email to team (Day 0): "Hi team — please welcome NAME, joining as ROLE on DATE. Buddy is BUDDY. Key meetings: M1, M2. Please add them to relevant threads and Slack channels."
- Manager 1:1 invite (Day 2): "Let's align on what success in 30/60/90 looks like. Please add any blockers and expectations to the doc linked in the invite."
- 21-day retrospective invite: "Quick 30m sync to review the first 21 days: wins, blockers, and immediate goals for the next 30 days."

Operational constraints and checks (people ops responsibilities):
- Ensure all compliance modules completed before any production access is granted.
- Coordinate with IT and security to automate environment provisioning where possible.
- Track onboarding progress in the central tracker (sheet id referenced) and flag hires with missing week-1 signals for escalation.

Appendix A — Example 21-day tracker columns (recommended columns for the sheet):
- Hire name, role, manager, buddy, start_date, week1_access_done (Y/N), week1_first_pr (Y/N), week2_independent_item (Y/N), week3_demo_done (Y/N), risk_level (low/med/high), notes.

Appendix B — Quick manager checklist (one-pager to paste into manager 1:1 template):
- Day 1: 30m intro and expectations.
- Day 3: Confirm access and security quiz completion.
- Day 7: First paired PR review.
- Day 14: Review runbook and handoff readiness.
- Day 21: Retrospective + 30/60/90 goals set.

Appendix C — Sample interview probes (copy/paste):
- "Tell me about a time you owned a service from triage to fix. What went wrong and how did you measure success?"
- "Walk me through a small feature you shipped recently and the trade-offs you made."
- "Here's a short code snippet (5–10 lines). What bugs do you see and what tests would you add?"

Open questions and items for follow-up:
- Convert mini-assignments into a standardized, low-friction repo pattern that doesn't expose production data.
- Work with security to create a temporary sandbox token flow for early staging deployments.
- Add a short multi-week survey to measure perceived onboarding clarity (sent at day 7, day 21, day 60).

Notes to implementors (people ops):
- This is deliberately compact and modular — local managers should not be punished for switching order, but required outcomes must be tracked.
- Keep the rubric examples short and concrete to reduce interviewer calibration drift.
- For hires with regulatory restrictions, insert mandatory compliance sign-off before any access.

End of doc.

- Week 1 signal: Has the hire completed required accesses and a first paired PR/runbook? (Y/N)
- Week 2 signal: Is the hire able to complete independent small tasks with limited review and update docs? (Y/N)
- Week 3 signal: Does the hire present a short demo and propose two measurable improvements? (Y/N)
Use these as inputs to the formal 30/60/90 check-in doc that goes into the HR file.

Communication templates (short snippets):
- Intro email to team (Day 0): "Hi team — please welcome NAME, joining as ROLE on DATE. Buddy is BUDDY. Key meetings: M1, M2. Please add them to relevant threads and Slack channels."
- Manager 1:1 invite (Day 2): "Let's align on what success in 30/60/90 looks like. Please add any blockers and expectations to the doc linked in the invite."
- 21-day retrospective invite: "Quick 30m sync to review the first 21 days: wins, blockers, and immediate goals for the next 30 days."

Operational constraints and checks (people ops responsibilities):
- Ensure all compliance modules completed before any production access is granted.
- Coordinate with IT and security to automate environment provisioning where possible.
- Track onboarding progress in the central tracker (sheet id referenced) and flag hires with missing week-1 signals for escalation.

Appendix A — Example 21-day tracker columns (recommended columns for the sheet):
- Hire name, role, manager, buddy, start_date, week1_access_done (Y/N), week1_first_pr (Y/N), week2_independent_item (Y/N), week3_demo_done (Y/N), risk_level (low/med/high), notes.

Appendix B — Quick manager checklist (one-pager to paste into manager 1:1 template):
- Day 1: 30m intro and expectations.
- Day 3: Confirm access and security quiz completion.
- Day 7: First paired PR review.
- Day 14: Review runbook and handoff readiness.
- Day 21: Retrospective + 30/60/90 goals set.

Appendix C — Sample interview probes (copy/paste):
- "Tell me about a time you owned a service from triage to fix. What went wrong and how did you measure success?"
- "Walk me through a small feature you shipped recently and the trade-offs you made."
- "Here's a short code snippet (5–10 lines). What bugs do you see and what tests would you add?"

Open questions and items for follow-up:
- Convert mini-assignments into a standardized, low-friction repo pattern that doesn't expose production data.
- Work with security to create a temporary sandbox token flow for early staging deployments.
- Add a short multi-week survey to measure perceived onboarding clarity (sent at day 7, day 21, day 60).

Notes to implementors (people ops):
- This is deliberately compact and modular — local managers should not be punished for switching order, but required outcomes must be tracked.
- Keep the rubric examples short and concrete to reduce interviewer calibration drift.
- For hires with regulatory restrictions, insert mandatory compliance sign-off before any access.

End of doc.

title: Org sync weekend brief + OKR stitching
preview:
Purpose:
This is a working weekend brief for a rapid cross-org stitch: consolidate individual team OKR drafts, produce a short exec-ready heatmap, and produce a concise all-hands narrative to land at next Tuesday's leadership sync. Not polished — treat as a scratchpad for Monday morning workshop.

High-level goals for this sprint (48–72 hours):
- Get aligned on 3 company-level objectives to carry into Q3 planning (one performance/cost, one reliability/platform, one go-to-market/expansion).
- Surface 6–8 target key results that are measurable and owner-assigned (prefer 1–3 KRs per objective).
- Produce a 6-slide all-hands narrative + 2-slide exec heatmap (risk/ask).
- Identify cross-team blockers and 5 concrete asks for leadership (budget/heads/priority).

Who should review this doc first: company strategy + heads of eng/product/platform + People Ops rep (for resource/HC asks).
Owners for sprint: Asha K (orchestrator), Priya N (data consolidation), Ethan C (ops heatmap), Marta A (all-hands narrative), Jonah L (jira/consolidation).

Meeting cadence this run:
- Sat 09:00–10:30 (async notes + pull in existing OKR drafts)
- Sun 11:00–13:00 (live stitch workshop — required for owners)
- Mon 08:30–09:15 (leadership dry run)
- Tue 09:00 (leadership sync; deliverables due 06:00)

Current constraints / assumptions:
- Budget envelope: assume neutral baseline; any incremental HC or infra needs must be justified via KR impact on revenue/operational risk.
- Hosted API capacity improvements have a soft launch planned in June — we should not overpromise latency/throughput KR improvements before dedicated infra confirms.
- Private deployment sales cycles are long; KRs should be staged (pilot -> contract -> install).

Draft company objectives (stitch candidates) — rough, open for edits:
1) Objective: Reduce inference unit cost and improve throughput predictability across hosted and dedicated offerings.
   - KR1: 20% reduction in average per-token cost on hosted API for top-50 customers (baseline Jan 2025).
   - KR2: Achieve 99th percentile latency <= 220ms for medium-length requests on dedicated SKU (p95 target already met; p99 is the gap).
   - KR3: Deploy two model quantization profiles and automated suggestion engine in Redwood Optimize that yield measurable cost savings for 3 reference workloads.
   Owners: eng-serving-runtime (+ product-optimize)

2) Objective: Increase reliability and graceful degradation to protect customer SLAs during peak events.
   - KR1: Implement regional fallback routing and automatic model variant failover, exercised in 2 staged chaos tests.
   - KR2: Reduce incident mean time to mitigate (MTTM) for production inference outages by 40% (tracked in SRE IRs).
   - KR3: Publish customer-facing SLA and rollout to top 20 enterprise customers with signed acknowledgment.
   Owners: site-reliability-engineering, platform-engineering, solutions-eng

3) Objective: Expand enterprise adoption of Redwood Private and Redwood Dedicated for regulated customers.
   - KR1: Close 3 new Dedicated bookings (annual committed revenue) with multi-region requirements.
   - KR2: Deliver an on-prem reference deployment and 1 completed pilot for a regulated customer (healthcare/finserv).
   - KR3: Reduce Private deployment lead time (from contract to production) by 30% through an enhanced onboarding playbook.
   Owners: go-to-market, private-deployments, solutions-engineering

Notes on metrics and measurability (quick guidance):
- Costs: use token-level breakdowns from the console and 90-day rolling averages to avoid noise from bursts.
- Latency: prefer p99 but include p95 as a secondary signal; record request-size buckets.
- Revenue/KPI: Dedicated bookings measured by ARR converted within quarter; pilots counted after a signed SOW.

Risks / Known unknowns (heatmap inputs for exec slide):
- Resource gap: SRE and infra teams flagged 2 unfilled senior ICs (RL-3410). If not filled by June, p99 SLO improvements may slip.
- Model qualification: upstream open-model changes could invalidate kernel optimizations; need an applied-ML buffer plan.
- Sales cycle lead times are longer than expected for regulated verticals; risk of KR1 under Objective 3 missing if pilot pipeline not matured.

Cross-org asks (leadership action items):
1) Approve temporary +2 headcount for SRE (contract-to-hire) to accelerate p99 work — justification attached in linked Jira RL-3421.
2) Greenlight $250k pilot budget for a healthcare private deployment PoC (covers infra + on-site support).
3) Prioritize Dedicated SKU billing/features workstream for Q2 release (product+billing).
4) Assign a single exec sponsor for the Private sales play (to unblock legal/KMS decisions).
5) Approve a 3-week hiring sprint (People Ops + Eng hiring partners) to reduce fill time on critical infra roles.

All-hands narrative rough outline (6 slides):
- Slide 1: Where we are — short health snapshot (usage growth, top-line hosted API metrics, Dedicated pipeline status).
- Slide 2: The challenge — cost and latency pressure as model sizes grow; our production priorities.
- Slide 3: Our goals for the next quarter — the 3 objectives above (one-line each).
- Slide 4: What success looks like — highlight customer stories, pilot wins, observable metrics.
- Slide 5: Asks & tradeoffs — where we need company focus and possible temporary slow-downs.
- Slide 6: People and culture — recognition + what's coming for hiring & onboarding.

Exec heatmap (2 slides):
- Slide A: Top 5 risks (ranked) with impact x probability, quick mitigation options.
- Slide B: Top 5 asks (HC & budget) with owners and expected timeline to decision.

Open questions to resolve in the stitch workshop:
- Do we anchor cost KR on per-token average or per-customer median? (Priya to propose sensitivity table.)
- Which Dedicated SKU do we commit to for the p99 latency KR? (Engineering to propose realistic SKU and test harness.)
- Can Legal fast-track the standard Data Processing Addendum for one pilot customer? (Marta to escalate if needed.)

Immediate next steps (owners + deadlines):
- Sat 18:00: Priya consolidates team OKR drafts into single spreadsheet (link: RL-3421) — ACTION: Priya.
- Sun 10:00: Ethan publishes risk heatmap draft for review — ACTION: Ethan.
- Sun 12:30: Live stitch workshop — all owners present — ACTION: Asha to run.
- Mon 06:00: Deliverables uploaded to exec packet (slides + heatmap) — ACTION: Marta/Jonah.

Appendix (quick pointers):
- Useful refs: Q3 roadmap draft (Confluence), incident history (SRE runbook RL-3399), current Dedicated pipeline spreadsheet (sales drive).
- Quick comms plan: short pre-read to all-hands 24h before event; two rehearsal runs (leadership dry run + full run).

Scratch notes / informal thoughts (do not include in slides unless polished):
- Consider surfacing a single customer quote about latency improvements vs cost tradeoffs; makes the story tangible.
- Potential cross-team co-invest: one-off infra optimization sprint in May that promises 8–12% cost upside; product to prioritize.
- If HC asks are denied, identify low-impact feature de-prioritizations to free effort.

If you are reading this on Monday morning: bring unresolved KRs to the stitch workshop and come prepared with a single numerical target and the acceptance criteria for each KR.

-- end of brief --

title: Company Reset (compact): OKRs, townline slate & all-hands run notes
preview:
Purpose
This is a compact, execution-focused reset doc for leadership and the all-hands agenda. Goal: land a tight, prioritized set of company OKRs for the next quarter, align cross-functional micro-asks, and run a pragmatic all-hands that surfaces risk and owners (not a status dump).

Audience
Leadership, PMs, Eng leads, GTM leads, People Ops, Customer Success. Expectation: owners arrive with one concrete ask and a single metric to track.

Executive summary (1 paragraph)
We need a short reset to lock the top 3 company priorities for the upcoming quarter and surface a small set of escalation points that could block delivery. This doc contains: a candidate compact OKR set, run-of-show for a 60-minute all-hands + 30-minute optional team syncs, rehearsal checklist for execs, and a matrix of micro-asks (what each org needs from others).

Proposed compact company OKRs (draft)
- Objective 1: Make hosted inference predictable and cheaper to operate
  - KR1.1: Reduce host API p95 latency by 15% for 95% of customer routes (baseline: Nov 2024)
  - KR1.2: Reduce average per-token cost by 10% across top 10 customer workloads (tracking via Console cost analyzer)
  - KR1.3: Deploy automated fallback routing such that cross-region fallback success rate >= 99%

- Objective 2: Expand Dedicated and Private product adoption for enterprise customers
  - KR2.1: Close 6 Dedicated pilot contracts with at least 3-month revenue commitment
  - KR2.2: Ship Private VPC onboarding playbook (self-hosted option) and reduce onboarding time to <= 10 business days for 80% of pilots
  - KR2.3: Complete SOC2 readiness checklist items flagged as P1/P2

- Objective 3: Improve developer experience for production customers
  - KR3.1: Launch SDK v2 with streaming + structured output improvements; adoption by 40% of active teams
  - KR3.2: Add first-class rollout primitives (canary + auto-fallback) and instrument 3 customers on early access
  - KR3.3: Reduce incident-to-resolution time for model-serving issues by 25% (SRE metric)

Why compact: we keep objectives to 3 and KRs to 2–3 each so teams can map and own them without diluting focus.

Micro-asks matrix (what we need from other orgs this quarter)
- Eng (serving-runtime): Need Product to prioritize A/B for caching policy; ask: freeze major API surface changes for 6 weeks during rollout window (owner: Priya).
- Product-hosted-api: Need SRE to approve new canary gating rules and provide runbook (owner: Morgan).
- GTM: Need Solutions Eng to draft three anchor customer case studies for Dedicated pilots (owner: Sofia).
- People Ops: Need hiring priority list for infra SRE and field AE roles (owner: Diego).

All-hands run-of-show (60 min core + optional 30 min breakout slots)
- 0:00–0:05 Opening (CEO) — north star recap, 1-sentence posture shift
- 0:05–0:18 Compact OKR pitch (Product + Eng leads) — 2 slides per objective: problem, metric, owner
- 0:18–0:28 Operational asks and risk register (SRE & Ops) — 3 highest risks, mitigation owners
- 0:28–0:36 GTM signal and customer telemetry (GTM lead) — one customer highlight, churn/expansion signals
- 0:36–0:44 People and hiring priorities (People Ops) — 2 hires that unlock objectives
- 0:44–0:54 Live Q&A (moderated) — pre-collected questions + 3 live questions
- 0:54–1:00 Close (CEO) — 3 micro-asks for teams (each team picks one)
- 1:00–1:30 Optional role-based breakout rooms (SRE, Eng, Product, GTM) for cross-team alignment

Run logistics and responsibilities
- Slide limit: 6 total (one slide per objective + one ops slide + one GTM + one People). Keep slides visual.
- Speakers must upload final slide deck to /commops/allhands by EOD two days before event.
- Mics: Host will use Zoom webinar; use moderated Q&A.
- Timekeeper: Chief of Staff (Rajan). Tech check: 30m before start.

Rehearsal checklist (for execs & leads)
- 3x run-throughs recommended for primary speakers (dry run, tech test, final).
- Confirm any demo or live playback works in spotty networks (record a short video as fallback).
- Everyone: bring a 15-second “ask” statement (what we need from teams).
- Prepare 1-line fallback messaging for the channels if an ask changes after all-hands.

Comms and cadence
- Pre all-hands: 1-paragraph email to company with 3 bullets (date/time, why attend, one-sentence summary of each objective) — send 72 hours before (owner: comms).
- Post all-hands: follow-up note with final OKRs, ask matrix, and links to breakout notes — send within 24 hours (owner: comms).
- Weekly cadence: owners publish a 2-line progress update in team channels every Monday (template provided).

Decision rubric for disagreements
- If a KR conflicts with resource constraints: ask for A/B tradeoff doc with impact estimate and required effort (2-week window).
- If two objectives contend for the same mandatory resource: tie-breaker via customer-impact delta (estimate of ARR or retention impact) + exec arbitration.

Risk register (top 5 immediate risks)
1) Host capacity shortage during rolling launch window — mitigations: temporary burst capacity allocation, staged rollout, fallback routing. Owner: infra lead.
2) Delayed SOC2 remediation blocking Private sales — mitigations: triage P1 items and assign dedicated engineer. Owner: security lead.
3) SDK v2 regressions in parsing structured output — mitigations: gated rollout + canary customers. Owner: devex lead.
4) GTM pipeline for Dedicated under conversion target — mitigations: fast-follow case studies + pilot discounting. Owner: sales ops lead.
5) All-hands overloaded with tactical updates — mitigations: enforce slide limit + use breakouts for tactical syncs. Owner: Chief of Staff.

Open questions and asks for leadership before committing OKRs
- Do we want to designate revenue thresholds that trigger escalation to executive steering (yes/no)? (decision needed by 2025-01-20)
- Can People Ops guarantee two prioritized SRE hires will be in pipeline within 6 weeks? (input due 2025-01-14)
- Will we open a limited early access sign-up for Private onboarding this quarter or hold to GA? (proposal due 2025-01-15)

Action items (next 7 days)
- By 2025-01-13: Product + Eng to confirm metric baselines for KRs (owner: Morgan).
- By 2025-01-14: People Ops to publish candidate job reqs for SRE + AE (owner: Diego).
- By 2025-01-15: Comms to draft pre-all-hands email and send to exec review (owner: Rajan).
- By 2025-01-17: Final slide deck uploaded to /commops/allhands (owner: leads).

Appendix: short templates
- Micro-ask template (one line): [OWNER] needs [WHAT] from [ORG] by [DATE] to unlock [KR/IMPACT].
- Weekly progress update (two lines): 1) Yesterday/today: short status; 2) Blocker/ask.

Notes to self / open fragments
- Consider adding a lightweight cross-functional ops booking calendar for rollout windows (maybe a shared airtable).
- Evaluate whether to present cost numbers as directional ranges vs exact dollars (legal review?).
- Add customer quote from pilot X as a highlight (Sofia to check permission).

End.

- By 2025-01-14: People Ops to publish candidate job reqs for SRE + AE (owner: Diego).
- By 2025-01-15: Comms to draft pre-all-hands email and send to exec review (owner: Rajan).
- By 2025-01-17: Final slide deck uploaded to /commops/allhands (owner: leads).

Appendix: short templates
- Micro-ask template (one line): [OWNER] needs [WHAT] from [ORG] by [DATE] to unlock [KR/IMPACT].
- Weekly progress update (two lines): 1) Yesterday/today: short status; 2) Blocker/ask.

Notes to self / open fragments

- Evaluate whether to present cost numbers as directional ranges vs exact dollars (legal review?).
- Add customer quote from pilot X as a highlight (Sofia to check permission).

End.

title: Panel orchestration + async debrief cookbook
preview:
Purpose:
This is a lightweight playbook for running distributed interview panels with asynchronous debriefs and an explicit day-one handoff. The doc aims to reduce panel cognitive load, speed decisioning, and create repeatable first-week experiences for hires.

When to use this cookbook:
- High volume hiring weeks where full live debriefs are costly.
- Cross-timezone panels with members in APAC/EU/US.
- Roles that need consistent day-one shadowing and fast offer cadence (IC and manager hires).

Quick summary (one-liner):
Run focused, timeboxed interview panels; capture structured async signals within 24h; publish a day-one activation snapshot so hiring manager and onboarding lead can pick up seamlessly.

Panel orchestration checklist (prep, 48–24h before interview):
- Scheduler: Confirm panel composition (interviewer role, backup interviewer) and timezone-aligned times. Use 30/60/90 minute templates depending on scope.
- Materials: Link the candidate packet: resume, coding assignment, portfolio, and 3 core prompts the panel will reference.
- Pre-reads: Share a 2–3 bullet role context with interview goals (e.g., \"system-design depth v. breadth\", \"culture fit anchors\").
- Tools: Ensure recording toggle off by default (policy), shared doc created (PanelDoc-<candidate>), and rubric template attached.
- Backups: Confirm 1 backup interviewer and escalation path (people-ops on-call).
- Candidate comms: Send calendar invite with interview flow, Slack contact, and what to expect on day-of.

Panel composition & role responsibilities (recommended baseline):
- Hiring manager (HM): domain fit, roadmap alignment, decision owner.
- Technical lead/peer: rigorous domain skill probe and system-level questions.
- Product/PM or cross-functional: product judgment, tradeoffs, stakeholder partnership.
- Culture/operations partner: process fit, team dynamics, onboarding acceptance risk.
- Observer (optional): note-taking, scoring consistency.

On-the-day script (30–60 minutes common pattern):
- Minute 0–5: Welcome + context from HM (team mission, 1–2 week ramp expectations).
- Minute 5–30: Skill probes (two focused exercises, timeboxed).
- Minute 30–45: Behavioral & cross-collaboration questions.
- Minute 45–55: Candidate Q&A; logistics (start date, compensation band expectations if pre-approved).
- Minute 55–60: Close and next steps.

PanelDoc template (what interviewers fill during/interview):
- Candidate name + role + ref link (1 line).
- Time zone + scheduled interviewer names (1 line).
- Live notes area (free text) — 1–2 bullets per interviewer.
- Rubric scores (below) + 1–2 line comment on score.
- Signal tags (drop-down style) e.g., [ownership | communication | execution | system-design | ambiguity].

Async debrief flow (must be completed within 24 hours):
1) Each interviewer posts their numeric rubric (0–4) and 2-line justification in the PanelDoc.
2) Hiring manager synthesizes composite recommendation: green/hold/red and 2–3 rationale bullets.
3) People-ops reviews for bias signals and missing reference checks; flags if additional live sync needed.
4) If unanimous green -> people-ops drafts offer template and shares to HM for sign-off within 8h.

Recommended rubric (compact):
- Technical mastery (0-4): 0 = no evidence, 4 = exemplary; comment required.
- Problem decomposition (0-4): ability to break ambiguous problems.
- Execution & follow-through (0-4): delivery track record + tradeoffs.
- Communication & cross-team clarity (0-4): stakeholder narration.
- Culture fit / values alignment (0-4): examples vs. defined team values.
- Overall recommended action: hire / hire-with-conditions / next-interview / no-hire.

Bias-mitigation prompts for reviewers (short):
- Is my language outcome-focused (\"achieved X\") or trait-focused (\"seems like\")?
- Would I hire this person for the concrete first-week deliverable?
- What evidence would contradict my initial impression?

Async debrief template (copy into PanelDoc):
- My 3 top signals (bullets).
- Rubric scores: Tech / Decomp / Execution / Communication / Values.
- Recommendation: hire / hire-with-conditions / next / no-hire.
- Short plan for day-one shadowing (if hire): 3 activities + 1 mentor.

Day-one activation snapshot (published immediately after offer acceptance):
- 5-line hiring-manager brief (focus, immediate priorities, blockers).
- Primary mentor + intro bullet points and calendar invites for week 1.
- Starter tickets (2–3 small, scoped tasks) with acceptance criteria.
- Access checklist: primary tools, code repo access, CI run access, Slack channels.

Common failure modes & mitigations:
- Slow debriefs -> set firm 24h SLA and a \"people-ops nudge\" cadence (12h).
- Sparse evidence -> require at least one concrete example per rubric axis or request a short follow-up 20m call.
- Timezone mismatch -> default to async rubric + 1 synchronous HM deep-dive.
- Divergent scores (2+ point spread) -> HM must summarize discrepancy and either schedule a 30m sync or request a short task-based tie-breaker.

Measurement (how we know it's working):
- Median time from final interview to offer decision (target <48h).
- Percent of hires with completed day-one snapshot before start date (target >90%).
- Panelist completion rate for async rubrics within 24h (target >95%).
- Qualitative: 30-day manager satisfaction with ramp (post-hire survey).

Templates & quick snippets (copy-paste):
- Candidate day-of calendar blurb: \"You are scheduled for a 45m panel with Redwood Inference. We'll introduce the team, ask 2 focused technical exercises, and leave time for your questions. Please join 5m early to check audio. Slack contact: @recruiting_ops.\"
- Async rubric prompt DM to interviewer: \"Please paste your 0–4 scores + 2-line comment in PanelDoc <link> within 24h. Use concrete examples where possible. Thanks!\"
- Day-one 3-task starter list example: 1) Run app locally; verify build (mentor: Sam). 2) Fix small bug in onboarding flow (mentor: Sam). 3) Ship doc with 1-page architecture overview (mentor: HM).

Open questions for iteration (notes to self):
- Should we require a minimum of one behavioral anchor per interviewer to improve values assessment?
- How do we integrate take-home assessments into the same async rubric without double-counting?
- Explore auto-synthesis helpers (small script that pulls rubric scores and formats the HM summary).

Appendix: quick calibration checklist for new interviewers (one-pager):
- Read role context (2 bullets) before interview.
- Use the rubric — don’t submit free-floating impressions.
- Ask for a concrete example when answers are high-level.
- Default to requesting a 20m follow-up if you’re unsure.

Last updated: 2024-11-15 (Ava Chen).
Notes: This is intentionally concise — copy into candidate folder when using.

title: Huddle Orchestration — Q3 Bridge & All-Hands Toolkit (draft)
preview:
Executive summary:

This is a working toolkit for the Q3 bridge period: a compact plan that ties exec updates, OKR drafts, and the all-hands run-of-show into a single orchestration playbook. Intended audience: execs, ops leads, comms, and TLs who will own runbooks and pre-reads.

Status: rough draft — we need input from GTM and Eng PMs on the customer-impact metrics; SRE to confirm incident tabletop schedule.

What this doc contains (quick nav):
- Three realistic scenarios for Q3 posture (growth / cautious / efficiency) with suggested executive statements
- Proposed company-level OKRs (3 primary + 3 supporting) with tentative owners and measurement notes
- All-hands run-of-show (30–40 min main stage + breakouts) + speaker checklist
- Pre-read and comms timeline (what goes to staff, when)
- Contingency / fallback tree for last-minute roadmap changes
- Decisions needed before Oct 25 (weekly exec sync)

---

1) Scenarios & recommended executive framing

Scenario A — Accelerate (baseline we want)
- Signal: paid conversion +20% from Q2, Dedicated pipeline closing ahead of plan, infra utilization healthy
- Exec frame: \"Double down where we have product-market fit while protecting experimental bets\"
- Key asks in all-hands: focused hiring in infra/serving-runtime (+10 role request), 2-week runway for performance sprint, greenlight canary for new routing policy

Scenario B — Measured Growth (most plausible given macro)
- Signal: steady usage growth, margins compressing, hiring cadence slows to critical roles only
- Exec frame: \"Prioritize customer reliability and cost efficiency; defer non-essential scope\"
- Key asks: freeze discretionary hiring except refills; accelerate Optimize features that cut token spend (batching recommendations); run targeted quality evals

Scenario C — Efficiency Mode (downside)
- Signal: enterprise renewals delayed, higher infra costs than expected
- Exec frame: \"Operational rigor, temporary cost controls, and clear roadmap triage\"
- Key asks: immediate cross-team cost audit, pause new pilots, surface trade-offs to customers where necessary

Notes: pick 1 scenario at the exec meeting, then craft a single short slide for the CEO and talking bullets for TLs.

---

2) Draft company-level OKRs (top-level, single source-of-truth: okr-redwood-inference-q3)

Objective 1 — Production reliability & customer trust
- KR1: Reduce P95 latency on hosted-api by 15% for 2–10k token calls (owner: Eng SRE)
- KR2: Achieve 99.95% uptime on Dedicated pools for top 5 customers (owner: Dedicated PM)
- KR3: Reduce mean time to detect/resolve Sev1 by 20% (owner: SRE lead)

Objective 2 — Cost/performance economics
- KR1: Lower per-token serving cost (USD) by 12% across hosted models (owner: Optimize)
- KR2: Deploy two quantized model profiles in production with validated evals (owner: Applied ML)
- KR3: Launch intelligent batching on 50% of queries by Q3 end (owner: Serving Runtime)

Objective 3 — Developer adoption & product velocity
- KR1: Increase active API keys (30d) by 25% (owner: Growth)
- KR2: Ship console-per-route observability features and onboard 10 internal teams (owner: Console PM)
- KR3: Publish 3 open reference apps that highlight latency/cost patterns (owner: DevX)

Supporting OKRs (short):
- Security/compliance: complete SOC2 gap remediation plan (owner: Security)
- GTM: close two Dedicated deals with >$100k ARR commit (owner: Sales)
- People: complete Q3 hiring plan + diversity milestones (owner: People Ops)

Notes: these are intentionally compact. Owners must add leading indicators and a 6-week check-in metric in Jira tickets (link to OPS-1182).

---

3) All-hands run-of-show (target 45 minutes + breakouts)

Pre-read (sent 72 hours before): 1-pager (CEO note), 2 slides: scenario selection + top OKRs, 1 appendix slide with cost metrics.

Run-of-show (timings are flexible):
- 00:00–02:00 (2m) — Housekeeping (Priya)
- 02:00–09:00 (7m) — CEO update: scenario + high-level ask (Ethan to prep 3 bullet talking points)
- 09:00–18:00 (9m) — Product demo: hosting + dedicated improvements (Maya)
- 18:00–26:00 (8m) — Reliability & infra quick wins (SRE rep)
- 26:00–34:00 (8m) — GTM customer highlight + commercial asks (Sales lead)
- 34:00–40:00 (6m) — People update + hiring ask (People Ops)
- 40:00–45:00 (5m) — Quick Q&A and next steps (panel)

Breakouts after main stage: 30-minute team breakouts (OKR working sessions). Each breakout has a facilitator and scribe.

Speaker checklist (TODO):
- Slide limit per speaker: 2
- One-sentence ask (clear action requested)
- TLs supplied with local TL notes + 1-line summary for their teams
- Rehearsal schedule: Oct 23–24 (booked slots)

---

4) Comms & pre-read timeline
- Oct 11: Exec selects scenario and finalizes CEO bullets
- Oct 13: Pre-read draft to all TLs for comments (48h comment window)
- Oct 16: Final pre-read distributed company-wide (72h before all-hands)
- Oct 22–24: Speaker rehearsals
- Oct 25: All-hands (target date)

Comms style: short, action-oriented. Use \"what we need from you\" framing. Avoid heavy technical detail in the CEO note.

---

5) Contingency / fallback tree (last-minute content shifts)
- If Dedicated deal news arrives day-of: swap GTM slot to 10m and push People update to breakout memo
- If major incident is ongoing: postpone public all-hands; run a smaller exec-only update + asynchronous pre-read summary
- If CEO unavailable: COO steps in with prepared bullets; ensure TL notes distributed

---

6) Risks & open questions (working list)
- We need precise margin impact estimates from Finance for the Cost/Performance objective (contact: Samir)
- SRE: confirm feasibility of the 15% P95 target in two months with current infra changes (status: needs data)
- Sales: confirm whether two Dedicated deals can realistically close within Q3 — impacts hiring ask
- People Ops: hiring timeline for infra roles vs. bench strength

Action items before next exec sync (Oct 18):
- [Ethan] Draft CEO slide + 3 talking bullets (due Oct 13)
- [Maya] Short demo script + 2 slides (due Oct 14)
- [Samir] Finance margin estimate + sensitivity table (due Oct 16)
- [Lena] All-hands logistics booking (rooms, stream, captions) (due Oct 18)

Notes to self / scratchpad:
- Consider a pre-recorded customer testimonial to reduce day-of risk
- Short internal survey after all-hands to capture clarity on OKRs (2 Qs)
- Quick scoreboard micro-site for OKR progress (mini-dashboard) — scope minimal: top KRs only

Appendix (links and quick refs):
- OPS-1182: central OKR ticket
- confluence:company/q3-bridge-prep — master pre-read (draft)
- slack:#leadership — open thread for TL questions

End of draft. This is intentionally concise — use comments in this doc for edits.

Unresolved: final selection of scenario (pick A/B/C) and finalization of the CEO ask line (one sentence).

PRIORITY DECISION: which scenario to communicate at all-hands? (vote/decision at exec meeting Oct 11)

--
